amiga:banshee.rationale: The AGA and CD32 releases use the same WHDLoad slave, but the current CD32 path has a reported controller failure while the AGA release works correctly.

amiga:chaosengine.rationale: The AGA release keeps the original gameplay while adding 256-color enhanced graphics; unlike CD32 it retains the main theme, and the WHDLoad patch supplies full joypad controls.

amiga:gunship2000.rationale: The CD32 release adds enhanced graphics, introduction, and sound, and the WHDLoad installer provides a distinct supported CD32 slave.

amiga:internationalkarateplus.rationale: The CD release is otherwise a straight port but maps the keyboard-only trouser-drop action to the joypad, making it the better controller-first Batocera version.

amiga:pinballillusions.rationale: AGA and CD32 use the same WHDLoad slave, but the CD32 game expects CD audio that is absent from the extracted PiMIGA package; AGA retains local in-game music data.

amiga:superfrog.rationale: The CD32 release preserves the full game and intro while adding controller-oriented two-button support; the current WHDLoad patch also saves high scores.

amiga:turrican.rationale: The CDTV release is the same game without enhancements and cannot save high scores; prefer the writable OCS WHDLoad release.

amiga:turrican2thefinalfight.rationale: The CDTV release has no gameplay enhancement and cannot save high scores; prefer the writable OCS WHDLoad release.

amiga:ufoenemyunknown.rationale: The AGA release has improved graphics and stronger review averages than OCS/ECS or CD32, while CD32 has a restrictive save limitation.
